summaries.summary: Identifies moDC_1 as a subtype of monocyte-derived dendritic cells in adult human skin. MoDCs are part of a differentiation branch distinct from LCs and myeloid DCs (DC1/DC2). Epidermal moDCs are HLA-DR+CD1a-CD11c+CD1c+ and are potent T cell stimulators.

Demonstrates that human dermal CD14+ cells are a transient population of monocyte-derived macrophages with a short half-life. Establishes the monocyte origin of CD14+ cells in human skin and their distinction from bona fide DCs.
Integrates adult skin dataset from Reynolds et al. (2021) including moDC annotations for cross-developmental comparison with prenatal skin.
